channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The 26-year-old man charged in last week’s killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO appeared in a Pennsylvania courtroom Tuesday, where he was denied bail and his lawyer said he'd fight extradition to New York City, where the attack happened. Luigi Nicholas Mangione was arrested Monday in the Dec.
item.dc:creator: SARAH BRUMFIELD and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: From wealth and success to murder suspect, the life of Luigi Mangione took a hard turn
item.description: Luigi Nicholas Mangione was apparently living a charmed one: the grandson of a wealthy real estate developer, valedictorian of his elite Baltimore prep school and with degrees from one of the nation’s top private universities.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DA suggests unusual idea for halting Trump’s hush money case while upholding his conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Eager to preserve President-elect Donald Trump ’s hush money conviction even as he returns to office, prosecutors are suggesting various ways forward — including one based on how some courts handle criminal cases when defendants die. Trump's spokesperson called the ideas “pathetic.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: House advances annual defense policy bill, teeing up final vote
item.description: The House advanced the annual defense policy bill on Tuesday, teeing up the mammoth measure for a final vote later this week. The chamber voted 211-207 along party lines to adopt the rule — which governs debate for legislation — for the sprawling bill, formally opening up debate in the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Military analysts break down national security concerns, doubt hobbyists at play
item.description: Military drone experts tell Fox News that SUV-size drones over New Jersey are not likely hobbyists and a foreign adversary could be responsible.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Susan Wild absent from Ethics Committee meeting after Gaetz leaks to press
item.description: Rep. Susan Wild (Pa.), the top Democrat on the House Ethics Committee, was absent from the panel’s meeting last week after being traced as the source of leaks to the press regarding the investigation into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Greene says she is safe after bomb threat
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) confirmed she is safe after reports swirled of an email sent to law enforcement containing a bomb threat targeting the lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Las Vegas judge dismisses infamous 'Black Widow' murder case after 27 years
item.description: The judge dismissed murder charges against Margaret Rudin, the woman dubbed "The Black Widow" who a jury convicted of killing her husband more than two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Video shows thieves speed off in sports cars from Michigan dealership
item.description: A group of suspected car thieves sped away from a dealership in rare sports vehicles, leading police on a high-speed chase in Caledonia Township.
item.dc:creator: Duncan MacLean, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump says tariffs will not hurt Americans' pockets
item.description: Trump was unwilling to speculate whether the costs of tariffs would be passed on to the American consumer.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows thieves speed off in sports cars from Michigan dealership
item.description: A group of suspected car thieves sped away from a Michigan dealership in rare sports vehicles, leading police on a high-speed chase that ended with the arrest of a 19-year-old Detroit woman.
item.dc:creator: Duncan MacLean, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Polygamous sect leader gets 50 years in prison in scheme to orchestrate sex involving children
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A polygamist religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives” including 10 underage girls was sentenced to 50 years in prison on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults, and for scheming to kidnap them from
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Jersey becomes latest state to prohibit bans on books in school, public libraries
item.description: PRINCETON, N.J. (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Gov. Phil Murphy signed a law Monday to prohibit public and school libraries from banning books in the state and to enshrine protections against civil and criminal charges for librarians who comply with the law.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Petition urges food allergy measures after Disney influencer's death
item.description: The petition calls on California lawmakers to institute new laws, including: a law that would make EpiPens a mandatory part of the emergency supplies at all restaurants or venues that serve food.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: Petition urges food allergy measures after Disney influencer's death
item.description: A Change.org petition calling on more food allergy measures in California has been launched after influencer Dominique Brown passed away after experiencing an allergic reaction to holiday food served at an event in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Mother arrested after posting video of herself kicking infant: police
item.description: A woman accused of kicking an infant multiple times has been arrested after she uploaded video of the abuse online, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing charged with murder, court records show
item.description: A man in Pennsylvania has been arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KTLA 5

item.title: Daniel Penny acquitted in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Daniel Penny found not guilty in subway chokehold trial
item.description: A Manhattan jury has reached its verdict in the Daniel Penny's trial in the death of Jordan Neely, who died after a May 2023 subway chokehold.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Polygamist with 20 'wives' to be sentenced for sex acts with children
item.description: A polygamous religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives ” including 10 underage girls faces decades in prison at his sentencing on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Woman, 52, accused of robbing Billie Eilish fans at knifepoint near Oregon arena
item.description: A woman was arrested for allegedly stabbing a man, then threatening and robbing a couple of their sleeping bag near the Moda Center Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KTLA 5

item.title: Juan Soto's $765M deal with the Mets is the largest in MLB history. Here's who he passed
item.description: Juan Soto and the New York Mets agreed to a record $765 million, 15-year contract Sunday night, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A timeline of the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson and search for his killer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The search for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson ’s killer since Thompson was ambushed Wednesday outside a Midtown Manhattan hotel has stretched beyond New York City.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Scottie Scheffler ends his big year in the Bahamas with his 9th victory
item.description:
Scottie Scheffler returned from a two-month break and nothing changed. He won the Hero World Challenge on Sunday with a 9-under 63 to tie the tournament record at Albany Golf Club and win by six shots.
Scheffler, the first player since Tiger Woods in 2009 to spend an entire calendar year at No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hemingway look-alikes visit Cuba and some of the late writer's favorite places
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Ernest Hemingway has returned to Cuba in a way. Eighteen white-bearded men who resemble the late U.S. author arrived in Havana to visit some of his favorite places when he lived on the island decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's 'Meet the Press' interview
item.description: President-elect Trump's first sit-down interview since winning a second White House term aired Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: US journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared in Syria in 2012, believed to be alive: Biden
item.description: “We think we can get him back," Biden told reporters at the White House, while acknowledging that “we have no direct evidence” of his status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Netflix must pay $385K to woman exposed as fertility doctor's secret child: court
item.description: A federal court in Indianapolis has ordered Netflix to pay $385,000 to an Indiana woman it outed as the daughter of Donald Cline, a former fertility doctor who secretly used his own sperm to inseminate patients.
item.dc:creator: Austin Hanson, WGN 9

item.title: 5 recipes from the pros to give after-dinner cocktails some holiday flair
item.description: From a festive Old Fashioned to a boozy hot chocolate, these after-dinner drinks might inspire your own menu of holiday cocktails, to be sipped slowly by the fire or washed down with your Christmas pudding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Check your junk mail: Some affected by the Equifax data breach to get more money
item.description: Customers affected by Equifax credit monitoring agency’s 2017 data breach may be eligible for another payment.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, Fox 5

item.title: Notre Dame opens to the public for first time since 2019 fire
item.description: Later on Sunday, an evening Mass will welcome members of the public who secured reservations last week.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Doorbell camera captures Illinois garbage truck explosion
item.description: Authorities described the explosion as "significant" and said truck parts were launched into the air, landing several blocks away.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, Fox 5

item.title: Florida prosecutor seeks to clear records of people charged with buying police-made crack in 1980s
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida prosecutor says he will seek to vacate as many as 2,600 convictions of people who bought crack cocaine manufactured by the Broward County Sheriff's Office for sting operations between 1988 and 1990.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disneyland announces closure date for Red Car Trolley attraction
item.description: Disneyland has announced a closing date for the Red Car Trolley attraction at Disney California Adventure as construction for an expansion of Avengers Campus nears.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WGN 9

item.title: Days after gunman killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, police push to ID him and FBI offers reward
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4
